observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management direct: 

The Municipal Accountant to observe the Perpetual Inventory Method by initially recording the purchases and receipt of office supplies, fuel, oil, and lubricants and other supplies and materials as Inventory, and subsequently recognize the same as expense upon issuance to end-users; and 

The GSO to prepare the SSMl or similar report showing that the subject supplies and materials were issued and received by intended users.
observations.reason: Revised/Restated on Audit Observation No. 7 in Part II of AAR CY 2024

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:

The MDC ensure that identified PPAs for implementation during the year are implementation ready to avoid delay in the implementation, moving forward; and

Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet been started and have remained unfinished as at year-end.
observations.reason: Revised/Restated on Audit Observation No. 9 in Part II of AAR CY 2024

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to require the suppliers/contractors to post warranty security for the procurement of goods in accordance with Section 62 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184, which shall remain effective for minimum period of three months to one year from the date of acceptance by the Procuring Entity of the delivered supplies, moving forward.
observations.reason: Revised/Restated on Audit Observation No. 8 in Part II of AAR CY 2024

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management thru the LCE to direct the municipality’s GFPS to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance.

We recommended, and the management agreed to record the assets at fair market value based on the latest available and reliable information such as Tax Declaration, and disclose such information in the Notes to FS.
Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the erroneous classification of transactions, and cause adjustment in the books of account to arrive at its corrected balance in consonance with the RCA.
Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare JEV to record the assets in the books the accounts affected as presented in summary of transactions under Appendix D.
Monitor the status of CIP projects and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for its completion.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the accrual of receivable from the waterworks system under the appropriate account.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that collections on hand are not treated as deposits in transit and properly reflected as Cash in Local Treasury at year-end, moving forward.
We also recommended, and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to record in the cashbook the actual collections and the actual deposits made during the day to arrive at the correct balance as of the given period to reflect the actual cash balance on hand.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant to correct and cause the necessary adjusting entry, as follows:
Semi-Expendable Agricultural and Forestry Equipment 83,346.00
Agricultural and Forestry Equipment		  83,346.00

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare a summary of all the RPTAR and Taxpayer’s Index Cards at the beginning of each year, based on the assessment roll, and furnish the Municipal Accountant for the set-up of current year receivables.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare and maintain the schedules to support the report balances of the RPT and SET Receivables upon receipt from the MTO;
Submit the basis for the adjustments made on the RPT and SET Receivables; and
Moving forward, prepare and submit to COA the JEVs on the current year set-up of RPT and SET Receivables, supported with the summary of RPTAR from the MTO.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed the following:
The Municipal Accountant to:
Record the purchase of supplies and materials for stock, including construction materials, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period as inventory following the perpetual inventory method; and
Record the issuances of inventories only when supported by SSMI with RIS and/or distribution list.
The Municipal Treasure/General Service Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the general process to be followed in the control of inventory as provided in Section 122 of MNGAS for LGUs; to properly prepare and maintain complete and updated SCs and SLCs; and to reconcile their records regularly.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the management agreed that the LGU refrain from making reference to brand names in the PR and to strictly observe the provisions set under Section 18 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed the following:
To procure its common-use supplies, materials and equipment (CSEs) from known bona fide suppliers, when not available in PS-DBM;
The BAC to ensure that the alternative mode through small value procurement does not fall under shopping; and
To strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 or the Government Procurement Law.
Recommendations
We reiterated our recommendation, and the BAC agreed to:
Provide a specific provision in the Notice of Award requiring the winning bidder to post performance bond and ensure that the winning bidders comply the same within the prescribed period prior to endorsement of procurement documents to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of Purchase Order; and
Strictly require the BAC-Technical Working Group to conduct and exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the Single/Largest Calculated Bid in order to properly determine whether the Single/Lowest Calculated Bid complies with all the eligibility requirements and conditions specified in the RIRR, of RA No. 9184.
Recommendation
We reiterated our recommendation, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the suppliers/contractors to post warranty security for the procurement of goods in accordance with Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184, which shall remain effective for minimum period of three months to one year from the date of acceptance by the Procuring Entity of the delivered supplies, moving forward.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed to:
Request assistance from the PSA in updating CBMS and/or GAD database and support in the interpretation of data gathered from an expert and/or statistician; and
Establish GAD database to contain SDD to serve as source of information for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GPB for costing.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed that LCE:
a.	Issue a directive to all LGU department/offices for the preparation of their annual GPB and annual GAD AR as early as January preceding the budget year; and
b.	Require the GFPS TWG to consolidate the gender issues and PPAs proposed by the different departments/offices, prepare GPB and submit the same the PPDO and the DILG Provincial Office not later than March 31 preceding the budget year.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the management agreed to refrain the practice of allowing the barangay treasurers to perform disbursing functions on their behalf especially to an AO who is not an employee of the LGU. Any advances made shall be drawn and accounted for under the accountability of the designated AO employed by the LGU and bonded for the said purpose.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the repair history of motor vehicles and heavy equipment is attached in the DVs;
The Municipal Treasurer to accomplish the pre- and post-repair inspection reports; and
Consider the designation of a GSO as it plays a vital role as mentioned above.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the management agreed to stop from granting any additional allowances, incentives, or other financial benefits to officials and employees unless expressly authorized by law.

We recommended, and the Municipal accountant agreed to;

Reclassify the balance of Due to PhilHealth account to Due to GOCC account.

Make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the entries made.

Properly review the transactions before posting in the books of account in accordance with IPSAS No. 1, COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and P.D. 1445.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Book up the earned income from the Waterworks system; 

Provide separate schedules of Accounts receivable to those unpaid balance; and

Require the officer in charge of the billing section to regularly provide the report on billed but unpaid service from Waterworks system to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording.

Therefore, in view of the accrual basis of accounting, this Office recommended and Management agreed to direct:

The Municipal Health Officer to regularly communicate and submit to the Municipal Accountant a Monthly List of Reimbursable Claims filed with PHIC; and

b.	The Municipal Accountant to:

Record in the GF books of accounts the PHIC reimbursable claims from PHIC upon receipt of the List from the Rural Health Unit (RHU) by a debit to the Due from GOCC-PHIC account and credit to Revenue accounts and refrain from accounting the receipt of payments from PHIC as trust fund; and

Make representation to the Philhealth that the LGU shall follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received from Philhealth for services rendered to patients with Philhealth coverage.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to properly present the Loans Payable due within one year as current liability in the Statement of Financial Position and its Notes to FS in compliance with the provisions stated in IPSAS 1 paragraphs 70 and 80.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to:

Provide a specific provision in the Notice of Award requiring the winning bidder to post performance bond and ensure that the winning bidders comply the same within the prescribed period prior to endorsement of procurement documents to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of Purchase Order;

Post the Invitation to Bid at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity concerned for seven calendar days as certified by the head of the BAC Secretariat of the Procuring Entity concerned per Section 21.1(b) of the RIRR, RA No. 9184; and

Strictly require the BAC-TWG to conduct and exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the Single/Lowest Calculated Bid (S/LCB) in order to properly determine whether the S/LCB complies with all the eligibility requirements and conditions specified in the RIRR, of RA No. 9184.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal GSO to notify the Audit Team of the deliveries of goods and services within 24 hours after its acceptance thru submission of AIR and its supporting documents such as purchase order or contracts, delivery receipts/sales invoice/charge invoice, and purchase requests, for proper evaluation thereof.

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF by preparing a DV and the Municipal Treasurer to draw a check and deposit the same to affect the transfer as at year-end, and not merely as set-up of obligation in the books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to:

Endorse to the Committee on Women and Family of the Sangguniang Bayan the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places; and

Include in the ordinance provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to adopt the following measure to Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet been started and have remained unfinished as at year-end,

We recommended that management thru the LCE to direct the municipality’s GFPS to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Book up the earned income from the Waterworks system;
Provide separate schedules of Accounts receivable to those unpaid balance; and
Require the officer in charge of the billing section to regularly provide the report on billed but unpaid service from Waterworks system to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording.

Therefore, in view of the accrual basis of accounting, this Office recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Health Officer to regularly communicate and submit to the Municipal Accountant a Monthly List of Reimbursable Claims filed with PHIC; and
The Municipal Accountant to:
Record in the General Fund (GF) books of accounts the PHIC reimbursable claims from PHIC upon receipt of the List from the RHU by a debit to the Due from GOCC-PHIC account and credit to Revenue accounts and refrain from accounting the receipt of payments from PHIC as trust fund; and
Make representation to the Philhealth that the LGU shall follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received from Philhealth for services rendered to patients with Philhealth coverage.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to properly present the Loans Payable due within one year as current liability in the Statement of Financial Position and its Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with the provisions stated in IPSAS 1 paragraphs 70 and 80.

We recommended, and Management direct:
The Municipal Accountant to observe the Perpetual Inventory Method by initially recording the purchases and receipt of office supplies, fuel, oil, and lubricants and other supplies and materials as Inventory, and subsequently recognize the same as expense upon issuance to end-users; and
The MGSO to prepare the SSMl or similar report showing that the subject supplies and materials were issued and received by intended users.

We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The MDC ensure that identified PPAs for implementation during the year are implementation ready to avoid delay in the implementation, moving forward; and
Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet been started and have remained unfinished as at year-end.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to:
Provide a specific provision in the NOA requiring the winning bidder to post performance bond and ensure that the winning bidders comply the same within the prescribed period prior to endorsement of procurement documents to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of Purchase Order (PO);
Post the Invitation to Bid at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity concerned for seven (7) calendar days as certified by the head of the BAC Secretariat of the Procuring Entity concerned per Section 21.1(b) of the RIRR, RA 9184; and
Strictly require the BAC-TWG to conduct and exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the S/LCB to properly determine whether the S/LCB complies with all the eligibility requirements and conditions specified in the RIRR, of RA 9184.

8.5	We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to require the suppliers/contractors to post warranty security for the procurement of goods in accordance with Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184, which shall remain effective for minimum period of three months to one (1) year from the date of acceptance by the Procuring Entity of the delivered supplies, moving forward.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal GSO to notify the Audit Team of the deliveries of goods and services within 24 hours after its acceptance thru submission of AIR and its supporting documents such as PO or contracts, delivery receipts/sales invoice/charge invoice, and purchase requests, for proper evaluation thereof.

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF by preparing a DV and the Municipal Treasurer to draw a check and deposit the same to affect the transfer as at year-end, and not merely as set-up of obligation in the books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to:
Endorse to the Committee on Women and Family of the Sangguniang Bayan the passing of an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places; and
Include in the ordinance provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to secure copies of turn-over documents and other pertinent records of the construction and heavy equipment and motor vehicle to support the JEV for recording of the assets and Grants and Donations in Kind accounts in the books.

Moreover, if securing of the required documents is not feasible, management shall conduct appraisal of the assets to determine the amount to be booked-up and facilitate immediate recording of the same.

In both circumstances, the transactions are to be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the Provincial Health Office to ask for the JEV of the donated vaccines and supplies and the same be communicated to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government.

Moreover, if securing of the required documents is not feasible, management to conduct appraisal of the vaccines and supplies to determine the amount to be booked-up and facilitate immediate recording of the same.

In both circumstances, the transactions are to be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:

1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct and /or restate the balances of affected accounts in the comparative financial statements in the ensuing year; and

2. Ensure that the necessary disclosures required in the implementation of the change in accounting policy are presented in the LGU’s Notes to Financial Statements.

We revised our recommendations that Management:

1.    Designate an accounts officer who will be assigned to transmit demand letters, communicate with the debtors and/or determine their whereabouts, in order to retrieve the necessary documents to support the filing of request for authority to write-off;

2.    Direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate and implement strategies on how to impose collection of the receivable accounts for those who are still capable of paying; and 

3.    Ensure that the Municipal Accountant expedite the filing of request for authority to write-off the remaining dormant receivable accounts after complying with the documentary requirements to support the request provided in COA Circular 2016 – 005.

We revised our recommendations that Management:

1.    Designate an accounts officer who will be assigned to transmit demand letters, communicate with the debtors and/or determine their whereabouts, in order to evaluate or assess the probability of collecting the accounts receivable;

2.    Direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate and implement strategies on how to impose collection of the accounts receivable; and 

3.    Through the Municipal Accountant and after determining the whereabouts of the debtors, and if warranted, undertake appropriate actions in filing the request for authority to write-off the receivable accounts after complying with the conditions and documentary requirements provided in COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that Management continuously commit the strict adherence to the existing relevant laws, rules and regulations on the proper utilization of the 20 per cent DF in ensuring maximum attainment of the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the Municipality.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to adopt the following measures:

2. Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet been started and have remained unfinished as at year-end,

3. Ensure that approved suspension orders are supported by relevant certifications or supporting documents; and

4. Craft detailed project monitoring and evaluation guidelines not just to ensure accountability and completion of PPAs within the timelines set but also to institutionalize best practices and interventions for infrastructure and non-infrastructure projects.

We recommended that management:

1. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to personally prepare the cashbooks for all accounts in accordance with the LGU-NGAs Manual; and

2. Reconcile with the Municipal Accountant at least quarterly

We recommended that management to:

1. Coordinate with the Bureau of Fire Protection, re-conduct evaluation and inspection on the municipal public market fire safety standards and implement fire preventive measures to avoid the risk of losing lives and properties in cases of man-made and/or natural fire calamities; and 

2. Provide the necessary and applicable firefighting equipment/ materials installed on conspicuous places, and post as well an emergency evacuation plan where anyone can likewise easily access.

We recommended that management fully institutionalize the LDRRM Office by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the LDRRM Officer in the administration and training and research and planning to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that management fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the LDRRM Officer in the administration and training and research and planning to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
We recommended that management avoid charging to the LDRRM Fund procurement of equipment which are not solely used for disaster related activities and observe the guidelines in the Circular.

Management to require the following:

Municipal Treasurer/GSO to:

Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE under COA Circular 2020-006;
 
Prepare all necessary reports in relation to physical count of PPE as prescribed by COA Circular 2020-006 using prescribed formats under the said circular or GAM for LGUs.

Conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties including those already dropped from the books without proper disposal in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations on disposal;

Prepare updated IIRUP after disposal has been made.

Municipal Accountant to:

Derecognize/drop from the books of accounts all unserviceable properties after disposal and ensure proper recording of the same in the books.

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian to:

Reconcile property and accounting records to ensure consistency of data/records.

Management expedite the filing of request for authority to write-off these dormant receivable accounts after complying with the documentary requirements to support the request provided in COA Circular 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendations that management:

Exert all efforts to determine the status and whereabouts of all the debtors;
 
Send demand letters to all debtors and ensure constant monitoring and follow up on the actions taken; and

Formulate and implement an efficient and effective collection strategy to fully recover the receivable balances as soon as possible.

We revised our prior year’s audit recommendations that Management:

Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year and quarterly period thereafter; and

Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted therein.

We recommended that management direct the:
GSO, through its disposal committee, to conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular 92-386, to prevent further deterioration and loss of additional income;
GSO to update the IIRUP after disposal of unserviceable properties and furnish copy to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team; and
MA to derecognize/drop from the books of accounts all disposed unserviceable properties after disposal and recognize gain/loss on disposal.

We recommended that management direct the:
MA and Treasurer to continue their efforts to determine the whereabout of the debtors and collect payments for those who are capable of paying; and
MA expedite the filing of request for authority to write-off the remaining dormant receivable accounts after complying with the documentary requirements to support the request provided in  Circular 2016 – 005.

We reiterated our recommendations that management:
Exert all efforts to determine the status and whereabouts of all the debtors to determine appropriate actions to be taken;
Send demand letters to all debtors and ensure constant monitoring and follow up on the actions taken; and
Formulate and implement an efficient and effective collection strategy to fully recover the receivable balances as soon as possible.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that management:
Facilitate the preparation of POW and other related documents for purposes of public bidding for PPAs indicated in the approved AIP;
Direct the designated monitoring committee to operationalize and activate the monitoring of the implementation of those identified PPAs of the 20% Development Fund from CY 2020-2021, including reprogrammed projects, and require them to submit monitoring report so that its causes of delays are adequately addressed; and
Assess if the PAPs can be fully attained if it’s under by Administration.
Management has committed to comply with the recommendations.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that management:
Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer (ME) to ensure that the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year and quarterly period thereafter; and
Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted therein.

We recommended that management thru the LCE:
Reconstitute the composition of the existing GFPS Execom and GFPS TWG; and
Create and establish a Monitoring and Evaluation Team in compliance with the provisions of Section 4.1.A.2. and Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to implement the GAD PPAs efficiently and effectively.

Management Comment

Management has conducted a municipality-wide survey on GAD-related statistics in July 2021, however the data gathered are not sufficient to conduct the gender mainstreaming and gender analysis on the determination of GAD PPAs. However, management has committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended management to:
Coordinate with the BFP, re-conduct evaluation and inspection on the municipal buildings’ fire safety standards and implement fire preventive measures to avoid the risk of losing lives and properties in cases of man-made and/or natural fire calamities; and
Provide the necessary and applicable firefighting equipment/materials installed on conspicuous places, and post as well an emergency evacuation plan where anyone can likewise easily access.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management:
Prioritize the close coordination among offices to carefully plan and review specific PPAs to be included in the AIP, set specific and realistic timeline for every program/project based on approved POW;
Create or designate monitoring committee to monitor the implementation of those identified PPAs of the 20 per cent Development Fund from CY 2020 and prior years, including reprogrammed projects; and
Reprogram those appropriations that cannot be attained or implemented and balances of those already completed; and
Require the designated committee to submit monitoring report so that its causes of delays are adequately addressed.

Management direct all offices concerned especially the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/GSO to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the receipt, utilization, recording and accounting of inventories for distribution as provided in the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs.

Municipal Treasurer/GSO provide complete data/information in the AIR and RIS and ensure that the said documents are numbered and dated for control purposes; and

Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of data or information presented in the AIR and RIS before attachment to the Disbursement Vouchers.

Municipal Social Welfare Development Office (MSDWO), who is tasked as the focal department in the identification and distribution of relief goods to beneficiaries, to ensure the submission of the distribution lists in a timely manner; and

Municipal Accountant to review the liquidations on the procurement of goods for distribution and ensure that the lists are attached to the DVs.

Management direct the:

Concerned department heads to require their official drivers to timely and regularly prepare and submit documents for the liquidation of fuel consumption monthly;

General Services Office to consolidate the prepared and submitted Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report for monitoring and control purposes, and to submit these reports to the Auditor for verification and evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption in a specific month; and 

Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provisions of GAM for LGUs which is effective in CY 2021 and onwards for the proper recording of purchases and utilization of fuel, oil and lubricants.

The Municipal Accountant

Review the validity of the accounts payable and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit (LGU), cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus. 

For those with valid claims, communicate with the creditors to confirm its existence and make appropriate actions.

We reiterated our recommendations that management coordinate with GFPS to:

Formulate strategies on how to gather sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information for the establishment of GAD database;

Assign a responsible person per office in gathering all the relevant information to be used in establishing GAD database; and

Designate a responsible person to collect and consolidate all the relevant information from the different offices to be used in establishing GAD database.

Management to:

Exert all efforts to determine the status and whereabouts of all the debtors; 

Send demand letters to all debtors and ensure constant monitoring and follow up on the actions taken; and

Formulate and implement an efficient and effective collection strategy to fully recover the receivable balances as soon as possible.

Management to coordinate with GFPS to:

Formulate strategies on how to gather sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information for the establishment of GAD database;

Assign a responsible person per office in gathering all the relevant information to be used in establishing GAD database; and

Designate a responsible person to collect and consolidate all the relevant information from the different offices to be used in establishing GAD database.

We recommended Management to require the following:
Municipal Treasurer/GSO to:
Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE under COA Circular 2020-006;
Prepare all necessary reports in relation to physical count of PPE as prescribed by COA Circular 2020-006 using prescribed formats under the said circular or GAM for LGUs.
Conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties including those already dropped from the books without proper disposal in accordance with prescribed rules and regulations on disposal;
Prepare updated IIRUP after disposal has been made.
Municipal Accountant to:
Derecognize/drop from the books of accounts all unserviceable properties after disposal and ensure proper recording of the same in the books.
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian to:
Reconcile property and accounting records to ensure consistency of data/records.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Treasurer/GSO provide complete data/information in the AIR and RIS and ensure that the said documents are numbered and dated for control purposes; and
Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of data or information presented in the AIR and RIS before attachment to the Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommended the:
Municipal Social Welfare Development Office (MSDWO), who is tasked as the focal department in the identification and distribution of relief goods to beneficiaries, to ensure the submission of the distribution lists in a timely manner; and
Municipal Accountant to review the liquidations on the procurement of goods for distribution and ensure that the lists are attached to the DVs.

We recommended that management direct the:
Concerned department heads to require their official drivers to timely and regularly prepare and submit documents for the liquidation of fuel consumption monthly;
General Services Office to consolidate the prepared and submitted Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report for monitoring and control purposes, and to submit these reports to the Auditor for verification and evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption in a specific month; and
Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the provisions of GAM for LGUs which is effective in CY 2021 and onwards for the proper recording of purchases and utilization of fuel, oil and lubricants.

We recommended that management expedite the filing of request for authority to write-off these dormant receivable accounts after complying with the documentary requirements to support the request provided in  Circular 2016-005.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management:
Exert all efforts to determine the status and whereabouts of all the debtors;
Send demand letters to all debtors and ensure constant monitoring and follow up on the actions taken; and
Formulate and implement an efficient and effective collection strategy to fully recover the receivable balances as soon as possible.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant
Review the validity of the accounts payable and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit (LGU), cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.
For those with valid claims, communicate with the creditors to confirm its existence and make appropriate actions.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management:
Prioritize the close coordination among offices to carefully plan and review specific PPAs to be included in the AIP, set specific and realistic timeline for every program/project based on approved POW;
Create or designate monitoring committee to monitor the implementation of those identified PPAs of the 20% Development Fund from CY 2020 and prior years, including reprogrammed projects; and
Reprogram those appropriations that cannot be attained or implemented and balances of those already completed; and
Require the designated committee to submit monitoring report so that its causes of delays are adequately addressed.

We revised our prior year’s audit recommendations that Management:
Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year and quarterly period thereafter; and
Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted therein.

We reiterated our recommendations that management coordinate with GFPS to:
Formulate strategies on how to gather sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information for the establishment of GAD database;
Assign a responsible person per office in gathering all the relevant information to be used in establishing GAD database; and
Designate a responsible person to collect and consolidate all the relevant information from the different offices to be used in establishing GAD database.

The Municipality to enhance its control and monitoring mechanism of outstanding checks by:

Sending written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the checks issued and to require the latter to return the same as basis of the Municipal Accountant for cancellation and adjustment in the books of accounts; and

Reconciling Municipal Accountant’s records and Municipal Treasurer’s records to reflect the actual cash balance on the effect of any adjustment made in connection with stale checks.

The Municipal Accountant to address the root cause of the difference by conducting reconciliation of balances between the reciprocal accounts. 

Thereafter, to make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances.

The Municipality to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA No. 2012-002 on the LDRRMF by:

Requiring the LDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP to be reviewed and updated annually;

Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF and submit to the COA Auditor thru the LDRRMO every 15th day of the month; and

Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account by year of transfer and provide proper disclosure of the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.

The Municipality to:

Expedite the conduct of relevant and necessary trainings or seminars to the collectors and other personnel involved in solid waste management;

Ensure to maintain an adequate number of personnel assigned in solid wastes management especially in the sorting of wastes for the timely segregation and disposal of waste; and 

Enforce strict compliance in wearing personal protective equipment on collectors, sorters and other personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste to protect them from the hazards of handling solid wastes.

Management to direct:

The Municipal Treasurer to:

Open a TF account for the 20 percent income from building permit fees and other charges and transfer thereto from the GF, the balance of the 20 percent share from the previous periods. Henceforth, income pertaining to the 20 percent share of the DPWH and OBO should be directly deposited to its TF account; and

Remit the remaining balance of the five percent of the DPWH to the BTr, if any.  

The Municipal Accountant to:

Record the 20 percent share in the TF books and maintain SLs to ensure proper monitoring of the shares of the DPWH and OBO; and

Maintain the corresponding SLs for the Permit Fees account.

The Municipal Building Official to:

Prepare a Program of Work (POW) for approval by the Local Chief Executive and submit the same to the DPWH Secretary thru the National Building Code Development Office (NBCDO) for the utilization of 15 percent share in accordance with the procedural guidelines set under DILG-DPWH JMC No. 001.

The management to require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.
The Municipal Accountant to make proper disclosure of road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipality enhance its control and monitoring mechanism on cash management regarding outstanding checks by:

Sending written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the checks issued and to require the latter to return the same as basis of the Municipal Accountant for cancellation and adjustment in the books of accounts.; and

Reconciling Municipal Accountant’s records and Municipal Treasurer’s records to reflect the actual cash balance on the effect of any adjustment made in connection with stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipality should address the root cause of the existence of payables of more than two (2) years by:

Requiring the Municipal Accountant to verify the claimants of various payables and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts without valid claims; and

Communicating to the creditors to confirm the existence of payables in order to make proper action.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter;

The Municipal Treasurer update the RPT Account Registers/Taxpayer’s index cards and submit the corresponding duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables; and

The Municipal Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipality enhance its control and monitoring mechanism of outstanding checks by:
Sending written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the checks issued and to require the latter to return the same as basis of the Municipal Accountant for cancellation and adjustment in the books of accounts; and
Reconciling Municipal Accountant’s records and Municipal Treasurer’s records to reflect the actual cash balance on the effect of any adjustment made in connection with stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to address the root cause of the difference by conducting reconciliation of balances between the reciprocal accounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances.

We recommended that management:
Exert all efforts to determine the status and whereabouts of all the debtors including those of the DSWD SEA Loans whose debtors are unknown;
Send demand letters to all identified debtors who are still alive and ensure constant monitoring and follow up on the actions taken by the debtors including those previously sent demand letters; and
Expedite the filing of request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, if warranted, after complying with the documentary requirements to support the request provided in  Circular 2016 – 005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management:
Exert all efforts to determine the status and whereabouts of all the debtors;
Send demand letters to all debtors and ensure constant monitoring and follow up on the actions taken; and
Formulate and implement an efficient and effective collection strategy to fully recover the receivable balances as soon as possible.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in retrieving the supporting documents of these liabilities to verify the claimants of various payables and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts if without valid claims; and
Communicate with the creditors of payables with valid claims to confirm its existence and make appropriate action.

The Municipality is recommended to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA No. 2012-002 on the LDRRMF by:
Requiring the LDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP to be reviewed and updated annually;
Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF and submit to the COA Auditor thru the LDRRMO every 15th day of the month; and
Requiring the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account by year of transfer and provide proper disclosure of the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipality:
Expedite the conduct of relevant and necessary trainings or seminars to the collectors and other personnel involved in solid waste management;
Ensure to maintain an adequate number of personnel assigned in solid wastes management especially in the sorting of wastes for the timely segregation and disposal of waste; and
Enforce strict compliance in wearing personal protective equipment on collectors, sorters and other personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste to protect them from the hazards of handling solid wastes.

We recommended that management to coordinate with GFPS to:
Formulate strategies on how to gather sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information for the establishment of GAD database;
Assign a responsible person per office in gathering all the relevant information to be used in establishing GAD database; and
Designate a responsible person to collect and consolidate all the relevant information from the different offices to be used in establishing GAD database.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Treasurer to:
Open a TF account for the 20 percent income from building permit fees and other charges and transfer thereto from the GF, the balance of the 20 percent share from the previous periods. Henceforth, income pertaining to the 20 percent share of the DPWH and OBO should be directly deposited to its TF account; and
Remit the remaining balance of the five percent of the DPWH to the BTr, if any.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Record the 20 percent share in the TF books and maintain SLs to ensure proper monitoring of the shares of the DPWH and OBO; and
Maintain the corresponding SLs for the Permit Fees account.
The Municipal Building Official to:
Prepare a Program of Work (POW) for approval by the Local Chief Executive and submit the same to the DPWH Secretary thru the National Building Code Development Office (NBCDO) for the utilization of 15 percent share in accordance with the procedural guidelines set under DILG-DPWH JMC No. 001.

We recommended that the:
Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) ensure that the list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details is prepared, maintained and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year; and
Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) who consolidate the quarterly status of accomplishments of all PPAs of implementing department and the Municipal Engineer tasked to oversee infrastructure project implementation coordinate with each other to prepare and submit the said quarterly report to the Office of the Auditor with complete details as provided in Annex B of COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make proper disclosure of road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that management verify the nature of the dormant accounts and revert the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be. 

Further, management should enhance its monitoring activity of its depository accounts and exert effort to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant. 

Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to close the accounts maintained with Philippine National Bank and transfer it to any of the authorized government depository banks.

We recommended that the Municipality should address the root cause of non – submission by:

Enhancing control procedures and monitoring in order to comply with reporting requirements;

Directing the concerned personnel to submit copies of duly perfected contracts within five (5) working days from perfection as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001; and 

Assigning personnel to monitor submission of contracts and other supporting documents related thereto to facilitate review and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipality:

Address the root cause of delay to ensure the immediate completion of the ADM on-going project;

Include anticipated unworkable days in estimating the number of days to complete a project to come up with a realistic project duration and avoid any imposition of liquidated damages on future undertakings; and

Require personnel of the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct regular monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that infrastructure projects are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract and to document the same and to impose liquidated damages equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of works for every day of delay pursuant to Item 8.1 of Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 if warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management should address the root cause of long-outstanding receivable and take action in the collection of the accounts on active employees of the Municipality by sending demand letters. If warranted, request for the write-off of the accounts as provided for in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant explained that on CY 2014 demand letters were sent to various accountable persons yet most of them still failed to settle their accounts. Management informed that they will take necessary measures to identify active employees and debtors then sending them demand letters to enforce payment of their obligations. In cases where collection is remotely possible, where the person liable is already dead or is nowhere to be found, management will require the Municipal Accountant to initiate action to request for write-off on those accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality enhance its control and monitoring mechanism on cash management regarding outstanding checks by:
sending written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the checks issued and to require the latter to return the same as basis of the Municipal Accountant for cancellation and adjustment in the books of accounts.; and
reconciling Municipal Accountant’s records and Municipal Treasurer’s records to reflect the actual cash balance on the effect of any adjustment made in connection with stale checks.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer explained that upon verification some of the stale checks as of December 31, 2019 where already cancelled and are now subject for reissuance. Moreover, for the rest of the checks, Management informed that will make verification and send written notices to payees if possible. If verification on the checks were to be found with no valid claimants, proper adjustment will be made on the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality should address the root cause of the existence of payables of more than two (2) years by:
requiring the Municipal Accountant to verify the claimants of various payables and make necessary adjustments on the respective accounts without valid claims; and
communicating to the creditors to confirm the existence of payables in order to make proper action.
Management Comment:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant explained that that these accounts payables are with valid claims which resulting from the adjustments of stale checks which remained to be outstanding. These accounts are ready to be paid to various creditors once demanded. Moreover, considering the time that these accounts have remained outstanding, management will make communications to various creditors to verify and confirm the status of claims of these accounts in order to make proper actions and adjustments on the books.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management to:
enhance PPA’s identification by the Municipal Development Council to identify projects, programs and activities that are workable and implementable within the year to get maximum value of fund utilization considering the increasing prices of materials;
institute control mechanism to address the root cause of delay or non-implementation; and
enhance monitoring control to facilitate timely corrective measure during the implementation cycle of the projects, programs and activities.
Management Comment:
Management explained that the first two projects as stated above were already started on CY 2019 and are already completed as of March 2019. Moreover, some of the allocations as stated in the 20% EDF are reserved for continuing balance for accumulation in order to allocate fund and reach the required amount for the project. Further, for the rest of the unimplemented PPAs under the 20% EDF for CY 2019, management will ensure its implementation and will do its best to follow the recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management verify the nature of the dormant accounts and revert the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be. Further, management should enhance its monitoring activity of its depository accounts and exert effort to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to close the accounts maintained with Philippine National Bank and transfer it to any of the authorized government depository banks.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer informed that by CY 2019 all three accounts maintained with PNB – Sindangan Branch will be closed and transferred to the AGDB account. Moreover, for the HYSA account under GF-LCSA, Management will communicate with the bank and comply with the documentary requirements in order to fully transfer the Bank Guarantee and to facilitate proper transfer and usage of these funds.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality should address the root cause of non – submission by:
enhancing control procedures and monitoring in order to comply with reporting requirements;
directing the concerned personnel to submit copies of duly perfected contracts within five (5) working days from perfection as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
assigning personnel to monitor submission of contracts and other supporting documents related thereto to facilitate review and evaluation.
Management Comment:
Management acknowledge the audit finding and promised to direct all the personnel concerned to coordinate with each other in order to facilitate submission of contracts within the required deadline. Management will seek necessary actions to implement the audit recommendations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that:
The Municipal Assessor submit to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Real Property Tax Assessment Roll which will facilitate in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter;
The Municipal Treasurer update the RPT Account Registers/Taxpayer’s index cards and submit the corresponding duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables; and
The Municipal Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Assessor explained that he was able to submit the assessment roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed with the corresponding assessed values of such properties last September 13, 2018 yet he also admitted that updated assessment roll that covers for October to December 2018 was submitted only on March 8, 2019. Municipal Assessor assured  to submit the required report with the reglementary period. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer explained that she based her RPT and SET estimates on last year’s collection efficiency due the continuous increase of tax delinquencies and that they might not be able to reach the required collection target as set by the Municipality for the budgeted year. But despite of the risk involved in setting up the RPT and SET estimates, the management will take necessary actions in order to comply with the recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality:
address the root cause of delay to ensure the immediate completion of the ADM on-going project;
include anticipated unworkable days in estimating the number of days to complete a project to come up with a realistic project duration and avoid any imposition of liquidated damages on future undertakings; and
require personnel of the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct regular monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that infrastructure projects are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract and to document the same and to impose liquidated damages equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of works for every day of delay pursuant to Item 8.1 of Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 if warranted.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Engineer explained that though the project incurred delays during the construction, the project was fully completed on January 24, 2019. Management promised that they will ensure monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that infrastructure projects are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract.

Recommendation:
The Municipality shall strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA No. 2012-002 on the LDRRMF by:
requiring the accountant to prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund and submit to the COA Auditor thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) every 15th day of the month; and
accelerating the filling –up of the required staff under the LDRRMO.
Management Comment:
For the submission of monthly LDRRMF utilization reports, the Municipal Accountant disclosed that she was able to prepare the report but failed to furnish a copy to the Office the Audit Team Leader. The Municipal Accountant promised to submit the monthly LDRRMF utilization report with the required period. For the accelerating the LDRRMO staff, the Management has already allocated for CY 2019 the required personnel staffing under the LDRRMO as required under RA 10121 and with the efforts of the personnel concerned, the management will do its best to fill-up the required staff for CY 2019.

Recommendation:
We have recommend that the Municipality enhance its compliance with RA 9003 by:
coordinating efforts as to integration of relevant costs for the implementation of the SWMP for consideration in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan.
providing the collectors with sufficient and necessary trainings or seminars to enhance their knowledge in the proper handling and disposal of solid waste; and
enforcing strict compliance in wearing personal protective equipment on collectors, sorters and other personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste to protect them from the hazards of handling solid wastes.
Management Comment:
Management acknowledged the audit findings and explained that as the date of exit conference, twelve (12) out of twenty-four (24) collectors have attended seminar on Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS). The management assured to take necessary actions in order to properly orient all their garbage collectors and sorters in order to enhance their knowledge in the proper handling and disposal of different kinds of solid wastes. For the proper wearing of protective equipment, the management will ensure strict compliance of the audit recommendation to protect them from the hazards of handling solid wastes.

We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer shall have to maintain Property Cards to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reconciliation of records.  Also, cause the disposal of unserviceable property by complying the required documents and procedures stated above.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that in the succeeding fiscal years:

The Municipality shall establish a separate allocation, not to be integrated from the other fund allocation expect from the 20% Development Fund, a one percent (1%) IRA allocation for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of their respective Local Council for the Protection of Children.

AAR

2017 | Conduct detailed evaluation and validation of document in order to avoid doubts in the process of process of procurement.  Ensure strict compliance with the rules and regulation sets forth in RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer shall have to maintain Property Cards to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reconciliation of records.  Also, cause the disposal of unserviceable property by complying the required documents and procedures stated above.

We recommend to management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance of the account Road Networks.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period.

We recommend that management shall execute a contract of lease between the municipality and the market stall occupants in order to effect the terms and conditions especially the payment of rent as stipulated therein that shall contribute to its collection effectiveness.

We recommend that the members of the Solid Waste Management Board to disburse only for programs and activities that falls within the ESWMP to ensure full attainment of the 10 year ESWMP which has the main objective of achieving 50% waste diversion by the end of 2017, full implementation of waste segregation at source in LUGs, to implement segregated collection system in LGUs, sustain waste collection services within the plan period, enforce mandatory composting at source within the 4th quarter of 2017 and to construct additional Material Recovery Facility.

We recommend that the members of the Local Disaster Management Office shall prepare Program of Expenditures for Mitigation Preparedness that will include a comprehensive preparedness plan/programs to institute measures for reducing disaster risks, including projected climate risks and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels.

We recommend that management shall conduct the gender analysis and sex-disaggregated data for the municipality to ensure that in the preparation stage of the GAD plan for programs, projects and activities shall truly address a well-defined gender issues towards the achievement of the Philippine Plan for Gender-Responsive Development.

We recommend that the management in the preparation of Gender and Development Plan will only include projects, programs and activities that truly address a well-defined gender issues towards the achievement of the Philippine Plan for Gender-Responsive Development.

We recommend that the Local School Board refrain from appropriating for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget especially those that are part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepED. Instead, it should allocate SEF for the operation and maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals, and sports development. Also, direct the district supervisors of the school to include the said activities in their budget proposal for DepED.

We recommend that in the succeeding fiscal years:
The Municipality shall establish a separate allocation, not to be integrated from the other fund allocation expect from the 20% Development Fund, a one percent (1%) IRA allocation for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of their respective Local Council for the Protection of Children.
We recommend that Pag-Ibig Contribution for both employee and employer share should be reduced to P100.00 per employee in compliance with Section 7 of RA No. 9679.

Proper coordination must be established between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation of the report on the P/P/As implemented ad to be implemented by the Municipality.

See to it that reported P/P/As are reconciled against the records maintained by the BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office before submission to COA for audit/validation.

Require the concerned official/Municipal Engineering Office to submit at the beginning of the year in accordance with the provisions of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and

Provide our Office of any corrections/revisions or changes to the projects to be implemented for our validation/monitoring.

We recommend that basic services/priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of the LGC should be considered in the implementation of the fund so as not to deprive the constituents of its benefit.

The LDRRMO must take all the necessary steps that facilitate local emergency responses through the implementation of P/P/As to reduce the level of risk or effects of disasters and geared towards the prevention of loss of life and reduction of loss of property brought by natural and human-induced disaster.

Coordinate with barangay officials, schools and private institutions in the formulation of disaster plan as to climate change adaptation in order to implement a well-comprehensive DRRMP that is holistic, integrated and proactive environmental impacts of disasters.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Pag-Ibig Contribution for both employee and employer share should be reduced to P100.00 per employee in compliance with Section 7 of RA No. 9679.

Recommendations:
Proper coordination must be established between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation of the report on the P/P/As implemented ad to be implemented by the Municipality.
See to it that reported P/P/As are reconciled against the records maintained by the BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office before submission to COA for audit/validation.
Require the concerned official/Municipal Engineering Office to submit at the beginning of the year in accordance with the provisions of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and
Provide our Office of any corrections/revisions or changes to the projects to be implemented for our validation/monitoring.

Recommendation:
We recommend that basic services/priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of the LGC should be considered in the implementation of the fund so as not to deprive the constituents of its benefit.

Recommendations:
The LDRRMO must take all the necessary steps that facilitate local emergency responses through the implementation of P/P/As to reduce the level of risk or effects of disasters and geared towards the prevention of loss of life and reduction of loss of property brought by natural and human-induced disaster.
Coordinate with barangay officials, schools and private institutions in the formulation of disaster plan as to climate change adaptation in order to implement a well-comprehensive DRRMP that is holistic, integrated and proactive environmental impacts of disasters.

We recommend that the physical inventory report should be reconciled with the balances in the books of accounts by the personnel concerned. This is to ensure the accuracy and validity of the balances of the fixed asset accounts reflected in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is almost finished but the adjustments will be taken up in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with the subsidiary ledgers of the accounting office and should be made at least at the end of each quarter to ensure the accuracy of recorded data in both records. Subsequently, the balance reflected in the agency’s financial statements of account Cash in Bank will be fairly presented and reliable.

We recommend that the LGUs SWM Board formulate their Solid Waste Management Plan that would complement to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Plan as provided in the Section 12 of RA. 9003 dated January 26, 2001.

Moreover, exercise their roles effectively in the capacity to perform being the agent of change particularly on the attitude of program implementation.
observations.reason: Ten (10) years solid waste management plan formulated (2017-2027) and adopted thru SB Resolution No. 2016-187 dated October 10, 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality should pass and develop a GAD CODE to serve as basis for identifying programs, activities and projects on GAD.
observations.reason: On the process of data gathering

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF. The LDRRMO shall then submit the said Report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC to the COA auditor of the LGU.
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant take-up the necessary adjustments/corrections of the affected accounts to provide accurate and reliable financial information.
